description:
A screenshot of a user interface dialog box titled "Select Vector Layers to Add...". The dialog displays a table with five columns: "Layer ID", "Layer name", "Number of features", "Geometry type", and "Description". Six vector layers are listed:
- Layer ID 2: LISTADO_ATRIBUTOS, Number of features: 21, Geometry type: None.
- Layer ID 0: LISTADO_COBERTURAS, Number of features: 65, Geometry type: None.
- Layer ID 1: LISTADO_USOS, Number of features: 86, Geometry type: None.
- Layer ID 5: SAR_15_T_COMBINADA, Number of features: 979728, Geometry type: MultiPolygon.
- Layer ID 3: SAR_15_T_POLIGONOS, Number of features: 2417938, Geometry type: MultiPolygon.
- Layer ID 4: SAR_15_T_USOS, Number of features: 1816094, Geometry type: MultiPolygon.
All "Description" fields are empty. The bottom of the dialog contains buttons: "OK", "Select All", and "Cancel". There is also an unchecked checkbox labeled "Add layers to a group" positioned between "Select All" and "Cancel" buttons. The "LISTADO" layers likely represent attribute or classification tables, while the "SAR_15_T" layers represent spatial vector data derived from Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery, possibly at 15m resolution, related to land cover and land use.
